zeus.description: Central orchestrator — delegates to specialized agents, never implements directly

athena.description: Strategic planner & agora — creates TDD plans and synthesizes multi-perspective recommendations for architecture decisions

themis.description: Quality & security gate — MANDATORY review after every implementation phase

hermes.description: Backend specialist — FastAPI, Python, async/await, TDD red-green-refactor

aphrodite.description: Frontend specialist — React 19, TypeScript strict, WCAG accessibility, TDD

demeter.description: Database specialist — SQLAlchemy 2.0, Alembic, query optimization, N+1 prevention

prometheus.description: Infrastructure specialist — Docker multi-stage builds, docker-compose, CI/CD

hephaestus.description: AI pipelines specialist — RAG, LangChain/LangGraph, vector stores, embeddings

chiron.description: Model provider hub — multi-model routing, AWS Bedrock, cost optimization

echo.description: Conversational AI specialist — Rasa NLU, dialogue management, intent/entity design

gaia.description: Remote sensing specialist — LULC analysis, satellite imagery, geospatial pipelines

apollo.description: Investigation scout — 3-10 parallel codebase searches, read-only, no edits

iris.description: GitHub operations — branches, pull requests, issues, releases, tags

mnemosyne.description: Memory bank management — ADRs, project.md, atomic facts to /memories/repo/

talos.description: Hotfix express lane — small bugs, CSS, typos, direct fixes, no TDD ceremony

nyx.description: Observability specialist — OpenTelemetry, LangSmith, token/cost tracking, alerting

argus.description: Visual analysis specialist — interprets screenshots, images, PDFs, diagrams, and UI mockups

agora.description: Multi-perspective synthesis engine — dispatches same question to 3-5 specialists in parallel, compares agreements & divergences, produces decisive recommendation

pantheon.description: Gather 3-5 expert perspectives in parallel and synthesize a single recommendation
pantheon.template:
Run a Pantheon synthesis on this question. Identify the 3-5 most relevant specialist perspectives, dispatch them in parallel with the same question, then synthesize a single authoritative recommendation.

Question: {{input}}

focus.description: Pin a session goal — all todos, delegation, and verification stay aligned with it
focus.template:
SESSION GOAL (pinned): {{input}}

All subsequent tasks, todos, and delegations must stay aligned with this goal. Verify alignment before each major decision. If a task drifts from this goal, flag it and ask for clarification before proceeding.

forge.description: Configure models by preset or model ID. Default = no models set. Presets in platform/forge.json.
forge.template:
Model declaration for Pantheon agents.

Read platform/forge.json for presets, tier mappings, and provider options.

If input contains '/' (model ID, e.g. opencode-go/deepseek-v4-flash):
  - Set root "model" in ~/.config/opencode/opencode.json to this model ID
  - Remove ALL per-agent model overrides
  - Tell the user: '1 model for all agents. Use /forge --agent <name> <model> for per-agent.'

If input is a known preset name from forge.json (e.g. "opencode-go", "openai", "github-copilot", "claude-pro", "byok-best"):
  - Read forge.json → presets.<name>
  - For each tier (premium/default/fast), map to agents via forge.json → tiers
  - Apply agent_overrides on top of tier defaults
  - Write per-agent model + variant to ~/.config/opencode/opencode.json agent.<name>
  - Also write provider options from forge.json → provider_options.<preset-name> into the provider section
  - Do NOT set root model
  - Validate JSON after writing: python3 -c "import json; json.load(open('~/.config/opencode/opencode.json'))"
  - Tell the user: 'Preset <name> applied. X agents configured across Y providers.'

If the user provides --agent flags (e.g. /forge --zeus opencode-go/kimi-k2.6):
  - Per-agent: write model to that agent's config in opencode.json
  - Only set models for the specified agents
  - Do NOT set root model

If the user types 'reset' or 'auto':
  - Remove root "model" from opencode.json
  - Remove ALL per-agent model overrides
  - Remove ALL provider model options (keep provider shells if they have baseURL etc)
  - Tell the user: 'All models reset. OpenCode will use account defaults.'

If the user types 'status':
  - Read opencode.json and show which agents have models set
  - If no models are set anywhere, show: 'No models configured. Using OpenCode account defaults.'

If the user types 'list':
  - Read forge.json → presets and show a numbered list with name + description
  - Read opencode.json → model to ...

subtask.description: Run a bounded child worker and return a structured summary
subtask.template:
Run this as a bounded subtask and return a structured summary when complete.

Task: {{input}}

Return format:
- Status: DONE | BLOCKED | PARTIAL
- Summary: 2-3 sentence result
- Files changed: list
- Next action: recommendation

sketch.description: Turn a rough idea into a structured spec through Q&A

mirrordeps.description: Mirror a dependency's source into .deps/ for local inspection
mirrordeps.template:
Mirror the source of this dependency into .deps/ (gitignored) for local inspection.

Dependency: {{input}}

Steps:
1. Identify the GitHub repo for the dependency
2. Run: mkdir -p .deps && git clone --depth=1 <repo-url> .deps/<pkg-name>
3. Confirm .deps/ is in .gitignore
4. Report the cloned path and key files to inspect

audit.description: Ask Themis to audit current changes for quality, coverage and security
audit.template:
Audit the current changes for quality and security.

Focus: {{input}}

Steps:
1. Run `git diff HEAD` (or `git diff --staged`) to get the change surface
2. Identify changed files and classify review scope level (Plan/Wave/Task/Final)
3. Run ruff on changed Python files; Biome on changed JS/TS files
4. Check test coverage on changed code (target >80%)
5. Run OWASP Top 10 security checks
6. Apply self-reflection quality gate (6 categories, 1-10)

Return: APPROVED / NEEDS_REVISION / FAILED with specific file:line feedback and the scoring table.

ping.description: Ping all 17 Pantheon agents — dispatches to each and reports status
ping.template:
Ping all configured Pantheon agents. For each one, dispatch a task asking them to reply with their name and status. Collect all responses and present a table with: agent_name | role | status (✅ Online / ❌ No response).

If any agent fails to respond within 10 seconds, mark it as ❌ No response and continue.

token-audit.description: Audit repo for token waste — find redundant context files, recommend optimizations
token-audit.template:
Run a token audit on this repository.

Steps:
1. Discover all AI context files (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, copilot-instructions.md, memory-bank, skills, agents, rules)
2. Build a redundancy map — what info appears in which files
3. Measure baseline: count lines, estimate tokens (1 line ≈ 4 tokens)
4. Identify red flags: historical content, derivable content, duplicated sections, files exceeding line limits
5. Generate a report with: baseline, redundancy count, red flags, recommended actions, projected savings

Present the report as a structured markdown table. If user approves, apply the optimizations.

praxis.description: Execute plan via task system - creates tasks from Athena plans, dependency ordering, mandatory gates
praxis.template:
Execute the plan using the task system.

Plan: {{input}}

1. Create tasks from the plan with dependency ordering
2. Execute each wave sequentially (parallel within wave, sequential between waves)
3. Stop at mandatory gates: Plan approval -> Phase review -> Git commit
4. Route: Zeus -> Hermes/Aphrodite/Demeter -> Themis -> Mnemosyne
5. Return status summary when complete.

metamorphosis.description: Intelligent refactoring - LSP analysis, Metis gap analysis, TDD refactoring, Themis verification
metamorphosis.template:
Run intelligent refactoring with safety guarantees.

Target: {{input}}

Steps:
1. Apollo: LSP analysis of target
2. Athena + Metis: gap analysis and plan
3. Hermes: TDD refactoring (RED->GREEN->REFACTOR)
4. Themis: verification (tests must pass before AND after)

Safety: No behavior changes. Tests must pass before AND after.

cancel-relentless.description: Cancel relentless mode - stops the self-referential development loop, preserves work
cancel-relentless.template:
Cancel relentless mode immediately.

Stop the self-referential development loop. Preserve all work in progress. Do NOT discard changes, close session, or delete tasks. Return control to the user.

stop-continuation.description: Stop all auto-continuation - stops relentless mode, auto-continue, and task system continuation
stop-continuation.template:
Stop ALL auto-continuation mechanisms immediately.

Stop: relentless mode, auto-continue, task system auto-continuation.
Preserve: manual tools, agent delegation, hooks, safety gates.

Return control to the user. Do NOT close session or discard work.
